MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-603704 WITH CASE CGC-21-591351
Off Calendar May-17-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. VAHEH MANOUKIAN, Esq. of the Matiasic Firm, P.C. (tel: 415-675-1089) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. STEPHEN SCOTT, Esq. of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson & Guslani LLP (tel: 650-637-9100) for Defendants HWA 555 OWNERS LLC; SO HUDSON 555 MANAGEMENT INC.; HUDSON WATERFRONT ASSOCIATES, I, LP dba HWA I, LP; HUDSON WATERFRONT I CORPORATION; HUDSONWESTSIDE ASSOCIATES, I, L.P.; VORNADO REALTY TRUST; VORNADO REALTY L.P.; and THE TRUMP ORGANIZATION.
Atty. MARK KANE, Esq. of Robinson DiLando, APC (tel: 213-229-0100) for Defendant Universal Protection Services.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT HWA 555 OWNERS LLC, HUDSON WATERFRONT ASSOCIATES, I, LP DBA, HUDSON WESTSIDE ASSOCIATES, I, L.P., VORNADO REALTY TRUST, VORNADO REALTY L.P., THE TRUMP ORGANIZATION, SO HUDSON 555 MANAGEMENT INC. AND HUDON WATERFRONT I CORPORATION MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-22-603704 WITH CASE CGC-21-591351.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant HWA 555 Owners LLC's motion to consolidate related actions for trial is denied.

Security guard Dan Jansen was attacked by a skateboarder while working at 555 California Street. Jansen sustained severe injuries and had portions of his skull and frontal lobe removed. In 2021, Jansen started a personal injury action against property owner HWA and others, CGC-21-591351. In 2022, HWA began a separate action against Jansen's employer, Allied Universal Security Services, seeking contractual indemnification, CGC-22-603704. Now, after two years of litigation and only six weeks before trial, HWA seeks to consolidate the trial of both actions.

CCP 1048 grants courts discretion to consolidate actions "involving a common question of law or fact" to avoid unnecessary costs and delays and the risk of inconsistent adjudication. Consolidation may be denied if cases lack common issues or if prejudice is likely to result from the consolidation. (Todd-Stenberg v. Dalkon Shield Claimants Trust (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 976, 978-979.)

Here, HWA argues that both cases involve the common issue of whether Jansen's injuries arose during the course and scope of his employment. To the extent this issue is genuinely disputed, it appears relatively straightforward, and unlikely to take up a large portion of either trial or result in inconsistent rulings. The remaining questions of law and fact in both actions appear largely distinct: HWA's alleged negligent exercise of retained control in one action, and interpretation of an indemnification agreement in the other. Further, HWA's delay in bringing this motion and its own choice to litigate the indemnification action separately cast doubt on the prudence of consolidating these actions now. For all these reasons, the court finds consolidation will not promote convenience, expedition, or economy.

Further, Allied has shown it would be prejudiced by consolidation. Consolidation would prevent Allied from conducting discovery to determine the operative indemnification agreement, given discovery is closed in the personal injury case. (See Kane decl. para. 11, 14.) As Allied is seeking its first continuance in the indemnification action, consolidation would also prevent it from filing dispositive motions. (See Kane decl. para. 15.)

Accordingly, the motion to consolidate is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling with a letter stating compliance with CRC 3.1312.
